title: Two Sum
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer target, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to target.
inputFormat:
First line contains an integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of three lines:
1) an integer n — the number of elements in the array nums.
2) n space-separated integers — the array nums.
3) an integer target — the target sum.
(Example for a single test case: n on line 1, nums on line 2, target on line 3.)
outputFormat: For each test case print the indices of the two numbers that add up to target in the format of a JSON-like array on a single line (e.g. [0,1]). If no valid pair exists, print [] for that test case. (Indices are 0-based as in the samples.)

title: Reverse Linked List
description: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list, and return the reversed list.
constraints: The number of nodes in the list is in the range [0, 5000]. -5000 <= Node.val <= 5000
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) an integer n — number of nodes in the linked list.
2) n space-separated integers — the node values in order (the linked list from head to tail).
(If T is 1 you can omit the first line and provide n and the list.)
outputFormat: For each test case print the reversed linked list as a JSON-like array on its own line, e.g. [5,4,3,2,1].

title: Trapping Rain Water
description: Given n non-negative integers representing an elevation map where the width of each bar is 1, compute how much water it can trap after raining.
inputFormat: First line contains integer n — number of bars. Second line contains n space-separated integers representing the height of bars.
outputFormat: Print the total amount of trapped water as an integer.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
description: Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a new sorted list.
constraints: The number of nodes in both lists is in the range [0, 50]. -100 <= Node.val <= 100
inputFormat: Two lines, each representing a linked list in space-separated integers.
outputFormat: Print the merged linked list as a JSON-like array.

title: Maximum Subarray
description: Find the contiguous subarray (containing at least one number) which has the largest sum and return its sum.
inputFormat: Single line with n space-separated integers representing the array.
outputFormat: Print the maximum sum of a contiguous subarray.

title: Climbing Stairs
description: You are climbing a staircase. It takes n steps to reach the top. Each time you can climb 1 or 2 steps. Find how many distinct ways you can climb to the top.
inputFormat: Single integer n representing the total steps.
outputFormat: Print the number of distinct ways to climb to the top.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
description: You are given an array prices where prices[i] is the price of a given stock on the ith day. Maximize your profit by choosing a single day to buy and a different day to sell.
inputFormat: Single line containing space-separated integers representing stock prices.
outputFormat: Print the maximum profit obtainable from a single buy-sell transaction.

title: Valid Palindrome
description: Given a string, determine if it is a palindrome, considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring cases.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 2 * 10^5, s consists only of printable ASCII characters

input.s: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

inputFormat: Single line containing the string s.
outputFormat: Print true if the string is a valid palindrome, else false.

sampleIO.input: A man, a plan, a canal: Panama

title: Search in Rotated Sorted Array
description: Given a rotated sorted array and a target value, return its index if found, else -1.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 5000, -10^4 <= nums[i] <= 10^4, all values are unique
inputFormat: First line contains n — number of elements. Second line contains n space-separated integers. Third line contains the target integer.
outputFormat: Print the index of target if found, else -1.

title: Container With Most Water
description: Given n non-negative integers a1, a2, ..., an, where each represents a point at coordinate (i, ai). Find two lines that together with x-axis forms a container, such that the container contains the most water.
inputFormat: Single line with n space-separated integers representing heights.
outputFormat: Print the maximum area that can be formed.

title: Longest Increasing Subsequence
description: Given an integer array, find the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence.
inputFormat: Single line with n space-separated integers representing the array.
outputFormat: Print the length of the longest increasing subsequence.

title: Valid Parentheses
description: Given a string containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.
constraints: 1 <= s.length <= 10^4, s consists of parentheses only
inputFormat: Single line containing the string s.
outputFormat: Print true if valid, else false.

title: Word Ladder
description: Given two words (beginWord and endWord), and a dictionary, return the length of shortest transformation sequence from beginWord to endWord.
inputFormat: First line: beginWord. Second line: endWord. Third line: space-separated wordList.
outputFormat: Print the length of shortest transformation sequence, 0 if impossible.

sampleIO.input:
hit
cog
hot dot dog lot log cog

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
description: Evaluate the value of an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation.
inputFormat: Single line with space-separated tokens.
outputFormat: Print the integer result after evaluating the expression.

title: Min Stack
description: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
constraints: Methods will be called at most 3 * 10^4 times
inputFormat: Operations are provided as an array. Each operation has an optional integer argument.
outputFormat: For each operation print the return value (null for push/pop).

sampleIO.input: MinStack, push -2, push 0, push -3, getMin, pop, top, getMin

title: Binary Tree Level Order Traversal
description: Return the level order traversal of a binary tree's nodes' values.
constraints: Number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 2000]
inputFormat: Tree is represented as level-order array with nulls for missing nodes.
outputFormat: Print the list of lists of node values level by level.

title: Kth Largest Element in an Array
description: Find the kth largest element in an unsorted array.
inputFormat: First line: n — number of elements. Second line: n space-separated integers. Third line: k.
outputFormat: Print the kth largest element.

title: Top K Frequent Elements
description: Return the k most frequent elements in the array.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 10^5, k is in range [1, number of unique elements]
inputFormat: First line: n — number of elements. Second line: n space-separated integers. Third line: k.
outputFormat: Print the k most frequent elements as a JSON array.

title: Course Schedule
description: Determine if you can finish all courses given prerequisites as pairs.
inputFormat: First line: numCourses. Second line: space-separated pairs representing prerequisites.
outputFormat: Print true if possible to finish all courses, else false.

title: Number of Islands
description: Given a 2D grid map of '1's (land) and '0's (water), count the number of islands.
constraints: m == grid.length, n == grid[i].length, 1 <= m, n <= 300
inputFormat: First line: m and n. Next m lines contain n space-separated characters '1' or '0'.
outputFormat: Print the number of islands.

title: Word Break
description: Given a string s and a dictionary of words, determine if s can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of dictionary words.
inputFormat: First line: string s. Second line: space-separated words of the dictionary.
outputFormat: Print true if segmentation possible, else false.

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
description: Given a string s, return the longest palindromic substring in s.
inputFormat: Single line containing the string s.
outputFormat: Print the longest palindromic substring.

title: Decode Ways
description: Given a string containing digits, return the total number of ways to decode it following mapping 'A'->1 ... 'Z'->26.
inputFormat: Single line containing string s.
outputFormat: Print the total number of decoding ways.

title: Coin Change
description: Given coins of different denominations and a total amount, return the fewest number of coins needed to make up that amount.
inputFormat: First line: n — number of coins. Second line: n space-separated coin values. Third line: amount.
outputFormat: Print the minimum number of coins to make up the amount or -1 if impossible.

title: Merge Intervals
description: Given a collection of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals.
inputFormat: First line: n — number of intervals. Next n lines: two space-separated integers per interval.
outputFormat: Print the merged intervals as a list of lists.

title: Jump Game
description: Given an array of non-negative integers, determine if you can reach the last index starting from the first index. Each element represents your maximum jump length at that position.
inputFormat: First line: n — number of elements. Second line: n space-separated integers.
outputFormat: Print true if the last index is reachable, else false.

title: Serialize and Deserialize Binary Tree
description: Design an algorithm to serialize and deserialize a binary tree. Serialization converts a tree to a string, and deserialization converts it back to the original tree.
constraints: Number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 10^4]
inputFormat: Tree is represented as level-order array with nulls for missing nodes.
outputFormat: Print the serialized tree string.

title: LRU Cache
description: Design a data structure that follows the constraints of a Least Recently Used (LRU) cache with get and put operations in O(1) time complexity.
inputFormat: Operations are given as an array. Some operations have integer arguments.
outputFormat: Return output for each operation (null for put).

sampleIO.input: LRUCache, put 1 1, put 2 2, get 1, put 3 3, get 2, put 4 4, get 1, get 3, get 4

title: Minimum Window Substring
description: Given strings s and t, find the minimum window in s which will contain all characters in t.
constraints: 1 <= s.length, t.length <= 10^5, s and t consist of English letters
inputFormat: First line: string s. Second line: string t.
outputFormat: Print the minimum window substring containing all characters from t.

title: Alien Dictionary
description: Given a sorted dictionary of an alien language, return the order of characters in the language.
inputFormat: First line: n — number of words. Next n lines: one word per line.
outputFormat: Print the order of characters as a string. If invalid, print empty string.

title: Subarray Sum Equals K
description: Given an array of integers nums and an integer k, return the total number of continuous subarrays whose sum equals k.
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) an integer n — number of elements in the array nums.
2) n space-separated integers — the array nums followed by an integer k on the same line or the next line (both formats acceptable).
(Example for single test case: n on line 1, nums on line 2, k on line 3.)
outputFormat: For each test case print a single integer — the number of continuous subarrays whose sum equals k, on its own line.

title: Permutations
description: Given an array nums of distinct integers, return all possible permutations. You can return the answer in any order.
constraints: 1 <= nums.length <= 8, -10^5 <= nums[i] <= 10^5, all elements are distinct
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) an integer n — number of elements in nums.
2) n space-separated integers — the array nums.
(If T is 1 you may omit the first line.)
outputFormat: For each test case print a JSON-like 2D array containing all permutations (order of permutations doesn't matter) on its own line, e.g. [[1,2,3],[1,3,2],...].

title: Rotate Image
description: You are given an n x n 2D matrix representing an image, rotate the image by 90 degrees (clockwise) in-place.
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of:
1) an integer n — size of the n x n matrix.
2) n lines each containing n space-separated integers — the matrix rows.
(If T is 1 you may omit the first line.)
outputFormat: For each test case print the rotated matrix as a JSON-like 2D array on its own line, e.g. [[7,4,1],[8,5,2],[9,6,3]].

title: Set Matrix Zeroes
description: Given an m x n matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0. Do it in-place.
constraints: m == matrix.length, n == matrix[0].length, 1 <= m, n <= 200, -10^9 <= matrix[i][j] <= 10^9
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of:
1) two integers m and n — the matrix dimensions.
2) m lines each containing n space-separated integers — the matrix rows.
outputFormat: For each test case print the modified matrix as a JSON-like 2D array on its own line.

title: Move Zeroes
description: Given an integer array nums, move all 0's to the end of it while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements.
inputFormat: First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines: 1) an integer n — number of elements in the array nums, 2) n space-separated integers — the array nums.
outputFormat: For each test case, print the modified array after moving zeroes as a JSON-like array on a single line.

title: Combination Sum
description: Given an array of distinct integers candidates and a target integer target, return a list of all unique combinations of candidates where the chosen numbers sum to target. The same number may be chosen unlimited times.
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) an integer n — number of candidates followed by n space-separated integers — the candidates.
2) an integer target — the target sum.
outputFormat: For each test case print a JSON-like 2D array containing all unique combinations (order within combinations and order of combinations doesn't matter) on its own line.

title: Pow(x, n)
description: Implement pow(x, n), which calculates x raised to the power n (i.e., x^n).
constraints: -100.0 < x < 100.0, -2^31 <= n <= 2^31-1, x is a double, n is an integer
inputFormat: First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of one line with a floating point x and an integer n separated by space.
outputFormat: For each test case print a floating point number representing x raised to n rounded or formatted reasonably (e.g. 5 decimal places) on its own line.

title: Search Insert Position
description: Given a sorted array of distinct integers and a target value, return the index if the target is found. If not, return the index where it would be if inserted in order.
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) an integer n — number of elements in nums.
2) n space-separated integers — the sorted array nums followed by the target on the next line or same line as per judge format.
outputFormat: For each test case print a single integer index on its own line.

title: Merge k Sorted Lists
description: Given an array of k linked-lists lists, each linked-list is sorted in ascending order. Merge all the linked-lists into one sorted linked-list and return it.
constraints: k == lists.length, 0 <= k <= 10^4, 0 <= lists[i].length <= 500, sum of all nodes <= 10^4
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of multiple lines:
1) an integer k — number of linked lists.
2) for each list, an integer n_i then n_i space-separated integers representing nodes of that list.
(If T is 1 you may omit the first line.)
outputFormat: For each test case print the merged linked list as a JSON-like array on its own line, e.g. [1,1,2,3,4].

title: Construct BT by Pre and Inorder Traversal
description: Given two integer arrays preorder and inorder where preorder is the preorder traversal of a binary tree and inorder is the inorder traversal, construct and return the binary tree.
constraints: 1 <= preorder.length <= 3000, inorder.length == preorder.length, -3000 <= node values <= 3000, all values are unique
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) an integer n — number of nodes followed by n space-separated integers — the preorder traversal.
2) n space-separated integers — the inorder traversal.
outputFormat: For each test case print the level-order representation of the constructed tree as a JSON-like array on its own line, using null for missing nodes, e.g. [3,9,20,null,null,15,7].

title: Rotate Array
description: Given an array, rotate the array to the right by k steps, where k is non-negative.
inputFormat: First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines: 1) an integer n — size of array, 2) n space-separated integers followed by integer k on next line.
outputFormat: For each test case, print the rotated array as a JSON-like array on its own line.

title: Sort Colors
description: Given an array nums with n objects colored red, white, or blue (represented by 0,1,2), sort them in-place so that objects of the same color are adjacent, using only constant extra space.
constraints: n == nums.length, 1 <= n <= 300, nums[i] is 0, 1, or 2
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) integer n — number of elements.
2) n space-separated integers — the array nums.
outputFormat: For each test case print the sorted array as a JSON-like array on its own line.

title: House Robber
description: You are a robber planning to rob houses along a street. Each house has a certain amount of money. Adjacent houses cannot be robbed on the same night. Given a list of non-negative integers representing the amount of money of each house, determine the maximum amount you can rob.
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) integer n — number of houses.
2) n space-separated integers — the amounts in each house.
outputFormat: For each test case print a single integer — the maximum amount that can be robbed, on its own line.

title: Product of Array Except Self
description: Given an integer array nums, return an array answer such that answer[i] is equal to the product of all the elements of nums except nums[i]. Solve it without using division and in O(n) time.
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) integer n — size of array.
2) n space-separated integers — nums.
outputFormat: For each test case print the answer array as a JSON-like array on its own line.

title: Linked List Cycle
description: Given head of a linked list, determine if the linked list has a cycle in it. Return true if there is a cycle, otherwise false.
constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^4], -10^5 <= Node.val <= 10^5
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines:
1) an integer n — number of nodes followed by n space-separated integers — the node values.
2) an integer pos — the index (0-based) where the tail connects to form a cycle, or -1 if no cycle.
outputFormat: For each test case print true or false on its own line indicating whether the linked list has a cycle.

title: Minimum Path Sum
description: Given an m x n grid filled with non-negative numbers, find a path from top left to bottom right which minimizes the sum of all numbers along its path. You can only move down or right.
constraints: m == grid.length, n == grid[i].length, 1 <= m, n <= 200, 0 <= grid[i][j] <= 100
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of:
1) two integers m and n — dimensions of the grid.
2) m lines each containing n space-separated integers — the grid rows.
outputFormat: For each test case print a single integer — the minimum path sum, on its own line.

title: Spiral Matrix
description: Given an m x n matrix, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order.
inputFormat: First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines: 1) integers m and n — matrix dimensions, 2) m lines each with n space-separated integers — the matrix rows.
outputFormat: For each test case, print the spiral order traversal as a JSON-like array on its own line.

title: Implement Trie (Prefix Tree)
description: Implement a trie with insert, search, and startsWith methods.
constraints: 1 <= word.length <= 2000 across all operations, inputs are lowercase English letters
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test batches. Each batch describes an operation sequence:
1) an integer m — number of operations.
2) m lines where each line contains an operation name followed by its argument(s) in a simple format.
(For simpler judges, provide operations and arguments as shown in samples.)
outputFormat: For each batch print a JSON-like array of results corresponding to every operation (use null for constructor) on its own line.

sampleIO.input:
2
7
Trie
insert apple
search apple
search app
startsWith app
insert app
search app
3
Trie
insert abc
search abc

title: No. of Connected Components in Graph
description: Given n nodes labeled from 0 to n-1 and a list of undirected edges, return the number of connected components in the graph.
inputFormat:
First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case consists of:
1) an integer n — number of nodes.
2) an integer m — number of edges.
3) m lines each containing two integers u and v — an undirected edge between u and v.
outputFormat: For each test case print a single integer — the number of connected components — on its own line.

title: Majority Element
description: Given an array nums of size n, return the majority element. The majority element is the element that appears more than ⌊n / 2⌋ times.
inputFormat: First line contains integer T — number of test cases. Each test case contains two lines: 1) an integer n — number of elements in the array, 2) n space-separated integers — the array nums.
outputFormat: For each test case, print the majority element on a single line. It's guaranteed that a majority element always exists.
